column1: What three elements must be combined to make a government truly democratic?
column2: Periodic free and fair elections, institutional rules and procedures, and the enjoyment of rights.

column1: In a democracy, what specific function do citizens' rights serve regarding the power of elected rulers?
column2: They set limits on what the government can do.

column1: About 600 people were secretly imprisoned by US forces in _____ near Cuba after the September 11 attacks.
column2: Guantánamo Bay

column1: Why were prisoners in Guantánamo Bay unable to challenge their detention in US courts?
column2: There was no trial before any magistrate in the US for these prisoners.

column1: Which international human rights organization reported that prisoners in Guantánamo Bay were being tortured?
column2: Amnesty International

column1: How did the US government respond to the UN Secretary General's recommendation to close the Guantánamo Bay prison?
column2: The US government refused to accept the plea.

column1: How is the ruler of Saudi Arabia selected?
column2: The country is ruled by a hereditary king.

column1: In Saudi Arabia, what role do citizens play in electing or changing their rulers?
column2: Citizens have no role in electing or changing their rulers.

column1: What is the status of non-Muslim religions in Saudi Arabia?
column2: Non-Muslims can follow their religion in private but not in public.

column1: In Saudi Arabia, the legal testimony of one man is considered equal to that of how many women?
column2: Two women

column1: Before Yugoslavia split, which ethnic group held the majority in the province of Kosovo?
column2: Ethnic Albanians

column1: Which Serb nationalist leader led the ethnic massacre against Albanians in Kosovo?
column2: Milosevic

column1: What was the final outcome for Milosevic after he lost power in Yugoslavia?
column2: He was tried by the International Court of Justice for crimes against humanity.

column1: What are the three essential qualities of a 'right'?
column2: Rights are reasonable claims recognized by society and sanctioned by law.

column1: Why does a right necessarily come with an obligation to respect the rights of others?
column2: Because a right is only possible when the claim is equally possible for everyone else.

column1: What gives a socially recognized claim 'real force'?
column2: When the claim is written into law.

column1: In a democracy, why do citizens need the right to express opinions and form political parties?
column2: To ensure that democratic elections can take place fairly.

column1: How do rights protect minorities in a democratic system?
column2: They ensure the majority cannot use its power to oppress or dominate the minority.

column1: In India, what are the rights that are given a special status and are fundamental to life called?
column2: Fundamental Rights

column1: What does 'Equality before the law' mean in a democracy?
column2: The laws apply in the same manner to everyone regardless of their status.

column1: Which concept serves as the foundation of any democracy and implies no person is above the law?
column2: Rule of Law

column1: On what five specific grounds does the Indian Constitution prohibit government discrimination?
column2: Religion, race, caste, sex, or place of birth.

column1: Why is job reservation for Scheduled Castes not a violation of the Right to Equality?
column2: Equality means giving everyone an equal opportunity, which sometimes requires special treatment for those in need.

column1: Which specific social practice was forbidden by the Constitution to end extreme discrimination based on birth?
column2: Untouchability

column1: Under the Right to Freedom, what are the six specific freedoms granted to Indian citizens?
column2: Speech/expression, peaceful assembly, forming associations, free movement, residence anywhere in India, and practicing any profession.

column1: What is the primary restriction on exercising the Right to Freedom in India?
column2: One cannot exercise freedom in a manner that violates others' right to freedom or causes public disorder.

column1: Is the freedom of speech an absolute license to instigate violence against others?
column2: No, the government can impose reasonable restrictions to prevent the instigation of violence or defamation.

column1: What is the constitutional requirement for depriving a person of their life or personal liberty?
column2: It must be done according to procedure established by law.

column1: Within what timeframe must an arrested person be produced before a magistrate?
column2: 24 hours

column1: What legal protections are granted to a person at the time of their arrest?
column2: They must be informed of the reason for arrest and have the right to consult a lawyer.

column1: Which three specific 'evils' are prohibited under the Right against Exploitation?
column2: Traffic in human beings, forced labor (begar), and child labor.

column1: Term: Begar
column2: Definition: A practice where a worker is forced to render service free of charge or for nominal pay.

column1: What is the minimum legal age for employment in hazardous work like factories or mines in India?
column2: 14 years

column1: What does 'secularism' imply regarding the state's relationship with various religions?
column2: The state remains neutral and impartial, without establishing any official religion.

column1: Does the right to propagate religion allow a person to forcibly convert others?
column2: No, a person is free to change religion only of their own will.

column1: Are religious instructions allowed in educational institutions managed by the government?
column2: No, there shall be no religious instruction in government educational institutions.

column1: Why does the Indian Constitution specify cultural and educational rights specifically for minorities?
column2: To ensure their language, culture, and religion are not neglected or undermined by the majority.

column1: In India, what constitutes a 'minority' for cultural and educational rights?
column2: Groups based on either religion or language that are in the minority in a particular area.

column1: Which fundamental right allows citizens to approach the Supreme Court directly if their other rights are violated?
column2: Right to Constitutional Remedies

column1: Which individual referred to the Right to Constitutional Remedies as the 'heart and soul' of the Constitution?
column2: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar

column1: Term: Writ
column2: Definition: A formal document containing an order issued by a High Court or the Supreme Court to the government.

column1: What is Public Interest Litigation (PIL)?
column2: A mechanism where any citizen can approach the court to protect public interest against a government law or action.

column1: What is the primary role of the National Human Rights Commission (NHRC)?
column2: To make independent and credible inquiries into cases of human rights violations.

column1: Who appoints the members of the National Human Rights Commission?
column2: The President of India

column1: Can the National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) punish those found guilty of violations?
column2: No, only the courts have the power to punish the guilty.

column1: The Right to Information was enacted by Parliament under which Fundamental Right?
column2: Right to freedom of thought and expression.

column1: To which broader right has the Supreme Court recently expanded the meaning of the 'Right to Life'?
column2: Right to Food

column1: Is the 'Right to Property' a Fundamental Right in India?
column2: No, it is a constitutional right but not a fundamental right.

column1: Which country's constitution guarantees a right to an environment that is not harmful to health?
column2: South Africa

column1: Term: Amnesty International
column2: Definition: An international organization of volunteers that campaigns for human rights and reports on violations worldwide.

column1: Term: Ethnic group
column2: Definition: A population whose members identify with each other based on common ancestry, cultural practices, or historical memories.

column1: The right of minorites to establish and administer educational institutions is part of which Fundamental Right?
column2: Cultural and Educational Rights

column1: Under the Right to Freedom of Religion, can the government compel citizens to pay taxes for the maintenance of a specific temple?
column2: No, the government cannot compel taxes for the promotion or maintenance of any particular religion.

column1: What must happen within 24 hours of a person being arrested by the police?
column2: They must be produced before the nearest magistrate.

column1: The practice of 'traffic in human beings' refers primarily to the buying and selling of people for _____.
column2: Immoral purposes
